name: Navarre (Navarra / Nafarroa / Foral Community of Navarre)
summary: Navarre (Navarra in Spanish / Nafarroa in Basque / Foral Community of Navarre / Comunidad Foral de Navarra) is a Northern Iberian civil-law foral community of the Kingdom of Spain — structurally distinctive globally as the only Spanish autonomous community holding the constitutional designation 'Foral Community' (Comunidad Foral) rather than 'Autonomous Community' (Comunidad Autónoma), and as the only EU member-state subnational jurisdiction operating its constitutional framework derived from a medieval Kingdom (Kingdom of Navarre 824-1620) continuously preserved through the 1841 Ley Paccionada (Bilateral Law of 16 August 1841) following the First Carlist War, the only Spanish jurisdiction with a bilateral-treaty constitutional foundation between Crown and Foral institutions. Navarre's Foral Civil Law tradition (Derecho Civil Foral de Navarra) derives from the medieval Fuero General de Navarra (~13th century) and was formally compiled in the 1973 Compilation of the Foral Civil Law of Navarre (Compilación del Derecho Civil Foral de Navarra / Fuero Nuevo). Navarre operates the Convenio Económico (Economic Agreement) fiscal autonomy framework distinct from but parallel to the Basque Concierto Económico — making Navarre the second EU subnational with full fiscal autonomy. Family-law framework operates under the Compilation of the Foral Civil Law of Navarre 1973 (as amended 2019 by Ley Foral 21/2019 — comprehensive Fuero Nuevo modernisation) Chapter VI Family Law, with parental authority and child custody provisions distinct from Spanish national Código Civil framework. The Tribunal Superior de Justicia de Navarra (TSJN) is the apex appellate court for Navarrese-civil-law matters; final appellate jurisdiction on Spanish constitutional questions lies with the Spanish Constitutional Court. Navarre is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Spain is a Hague Convention 1980 party (acceded 16 June 1987) — Navarrese Hague applicability via Spanish federal extension subject to Navarrese Foral Civil Law application framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Compilación del Derecho Civil Foral de Navarra (Fuero Nuevo) 1973 as amended 2019
statutory_framework.title: Compilation of the Foral Civil Law of Navarre (Fuero Nuevo)
statutory_framework.relevance: Compilation of Navarrese foral civil law originally adopted 1 March 1973 — comprehensively modernised by Ley Foral 21/2019 of 4 April 2019. Governs family law, succession, and property law distinct from Spanish national Código Civil framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Ley Orgánica de Reintegración y Amejoramiento del Régimen Foral de Navarra 1982 (Statute of Autonomy)
statutory_framework.title: Statute of Reintegration and Amejoramiento of the Foral Regime of Navarre
statutory_framework.relevance: Spanish Organic Law establishing Navarrese foral-community governance framework — distinctively styled 'Amejoramiento' (improvement) of the Foral Regime, reflecting the unique constitutional foundation in pre-existing Foral institutions.

statutory_framework.title: Convenio Económico
statutory_framework.relevance: Navarrese fiscal autonomy framework — second EU subnational with full fiscal autonomy alongside Basque Concierto Económico.

statutory_framework.title: Ley Paccionada 1841
statutory_framework.relevance: Spanish Bilateral Law of 16 August 1841 between Crown and Foral institutions following First Carlist War — constitutional foundation for Navarrese bilateral-treaty foral framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Spanish Constitution 1978 First Additional Provision (Foral rights)
statutory_framework.title: Spanish Constitution First Additional Provision
statutory_framework.relevance: Spanish Constitutional Provision recognising the rights of the historical territories of Navarre and the Basque Country.

apex_courts.name: Tribunal Superior de Justicia de Navarra (TSJN)
apex_courts.role: Apex appellate court for Navarrese-foral-civil-law matters from Navarre.

apex_courts.name: Tribunal Constitucional de España
apex_courts.role: Apex Spanish constitutional court for federal constitutional questions from Navarre.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio Oficial de la Psicología de Navarra
professional_regulators.role: Navarrese foral-community professional regulatory framework for psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Navarrese family-court decisions are anonymised per Navarrese court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Kingdom of Pamplona / Kingdom of Navarre founded
key_developments.description: Kingdom of Pamplona (later Kingdom of Navarre) founded by Íñigo Arista in 824 — foundation of medieval Navarrese state + structurally distinctive globally only EU member-state subnational jurisdiction operating its constitutional framework derived from a medieval Kingdom (824-1620) continuously preserved.

key_developments.title: Fuero General de Navarra
key_developments.description: Fuero General de Navarra ~13th century — foundational compilation of Navarrese customary law + foundation for subsequent Fuero Nuevo 1973 framework + pivotal medieval foundation framework predating Castilian conquest framework.

key_developments.title: Castilian conquest of Upper Navarre
key_developments.description: Castilian conquest of Upper Navarre 1512-1521 by Fernando the Catholic following 1512 Navarrese-French alliance during War of the League of Cambrai — incorporation into Crown of Castile with Foral autonomy preservation + Lower Navarre remained under French Navarrese monarchy 1512-1620 (subsequently united with French crown via Henry IV).

key_developments.title: Lower Navarre Union with French Crown
key_developments.description: Lower Navarre formally united with French Crown 20 October 1620 by King Louis XIII — definitive end of Kingdom of Navarre as separate sovereign entity + pivotal end of ~800-year sovereign Navarrese monarchy framework + foundation for subsequent Upper Navarre Foral preservation framework under Spanish Crown.

key_developments.title: Ley Paccionada (Bilateral Law)
key_developments.description: Spanish Bilateral Law of 16 August 1841 between Crown and Foral institutions following First Carlist War (1833-1840) — constitutional foundation for Navarrese bilateral-treaty foral framework + structurally distinctive globally only Spanish jurisdiction with bilateral-treaty constitutional foundation between Crown and Foral institutions.

key_developments.title: Compilación del Derecho Civil Foral de Navarra (Fuero Nuevo)
key_developments.description: Compilation of Navarrese foral civil law (Fuero Nuevo) adopted 1 March 1973 under Franco regime — first modern formal codification of Navarrese foral civil law + foundation for subsequent 2019 Ley Foral 21/2019 modernisation framework.

key_developments.title: Statute of Autonomy + Amejoramiento del Régimen Foral
key_developments.description: Spanish Organic Law 13/1982 of 10 August 1982 establishing Navarrese foral-community governance framework — distinctively styled 'Amejoramiento' (improvement) of the Foral Regime rather than 'Statute of Autonomy' + structurally distinctive globally only Spanish autonomous community holding constitutional designation 'Foral Community' (Comunidad Foral) rather than 'Autonomous Community'.

key_developments.title: Convenio Económico fiscal autonomy framework
key_developments.description: Convenio Económico (Navarrese Economic Agreement) 1990 establishing Navarrese full fiscal autonomy framework — second EU subnational with full fiscal autonomy alongside Basque Concierto Económico + pivotal modern Navarrese fiscal-autonomy framework foundation.

key_developments.title: Comprehensive Fuero Nuevo modernisation
key_developments.description: Ley Foral 21/2019 of 4 April 2019 comprehensively modernising Compilation of Navarrese foral civil law — most recent major modernisation including updated family-law-adjacent provisions + Chapter VI Family Law modernisation distinct from Spanish national Código Civil framework.

key_developments.title: María Chivite PSN-PSOE Government + Parlamento-de-Navarra + Defensor-del-Pueblo + EH-Bildu-coalition framework
key_developments.description: María Chivite (PSN-PSOE) President framework continuing 2023-onwards with Parlamento de Navarra institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Compilación del Derecho Civil Foral de Navarra (Fuero Nuevo) Chapter VI Family Law framework applied via Tribunal Superior de Justicia de Navarra. EH-Bildu-Geroa-Bai coalition support framework substantively significant institutional context. Defensor del Pueblo de Navarra institutional positions affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Substantively significant Northern Iberian foral-community institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Continuing Foral Community framework + medieval-derived constitutional continuity
key_developments.description: Continuing Navarrese Foral Community framework 2024 — continuing Comunidad Foral constitutional designation + continuing Amejoramiento del Régimen Foral framework + continuing Convenio Económico fiscal autonomy + continuing Fuero Nuevo 1973-2019 framework + continuing bilateral-treaty Ley Paccionada 1841 constitutional foundation + 1,200 years since Kingdom of Pamplona/Navarre foundation 824.

structural_findings: Navarre operates a civil-law framework with own Foral Civil Law jurisdiction — places Navarre in the EU subnational civil-law-with-own-code cluster.
Only Spanish autonomous community holding the constitutional designation 'Foral Community' (Comunidad Foral) rather than 'Autonomous Community' is structurally distinctive globally.

Only EU member-state subnational jurisdiction operating its constitutional framework derived from a medieval Kingdom (Kingdom of Navarre 824-1620) continuously preserved through bilateral-treaty constitutional foundation is structurally distinctive globally — Ley Paccionada 1841.
Second EU subnational with full fiscal autonomy (Convenio Económico) alongside Basque Concierto Económico is structurally distinctive globally.
Compilation of Navarrese foral civil law (Fuero Nuevo) 1973 modernised 2019 is structurally distinctive within European subnational civil-code cluster.

Statute styled 'Amejoramiento del Régimen Foral' (Improvement of the Foral Regime) rather than 'Statute of Autonomy' is structurally distinctive globally — reflecting unique constitutional foundation in pre-existing Foral institutions rather than newly-created autonomous framework.
Spanish Constitution First Additional Provision (Foral rights) recognition is shared with Basque Country — only two EU subnationals with constitutional Foral rights recognition.
Spanish Hague Convention 1980 accession 1987 + Navarrese Foral Civil Law application framework is structurally distinctive.

sources.title: Tribunal Superior de Justicia de Navarra

editorial_notes: Navarre jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — civil-law Northern Iberian Spanish foral community with own Navarrese foral civil-law jurisdiction (824 Kingdom of Pamplona/Navarre + 1238 Fuero General de Navarra + 1512 Castilian conquest Upper Navarre + 1620 Lower Navarre Union with French Crown + 1841 Ley Paccionada bilateral-treaty + 1973 Compilación Fuero Nuevo + 1982 Amejoramiento Régimen Foral + 1990 Convenio Económico fiscal autonomy + 2019 Fuero Nuevo modernisation + 2024 continuing 1,200-year medieval-derived framework). Only Spanish autonomous community holding constitutional designation Foral Community globally + only EU member-state subnational operating constitutional framework derived from medieval Kingdom continuously preserved through bilateral-treaty + second EU subnational with full fiscal autonomy + Amejoramiento del Régimen Foral constitutional framing distinct from Statute of Autonomy + 1,200 years since Kingdom of Pamplona/Navarre foundation.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Northern-Iberian + civil-law + EU-subnational-civil-law-with-own-code cluster + Foral-Community-constitutional-designation-globally-distinctive + medieval-Kingdom-derived-constitutional-framework + bilateral-treaty-Ley-Paccionada-1841-foundation + Convenio-Económico-full-fiscal-autonomy + Amejoramiento-del-Régimen-Foral-constitutional-framing + Fuero-Nuevo-1973-2019 + Spanish-Constitution-First-Additional-Provision-Foral-rights clusters within the corpus.
